# BYD Seal 08 hits 65,000 orders in just over 30 hours with 694 hp dual motors

**3 min to read**

Jul 4, 2026 2:37 PM CEST

[Adrian Leung](https://carnewschina.com/author/adri_lng_007/)
[2](#comments-262426)

BYD accumulated thousands of customer orders for the Seal 08 sedan within just over 30 hours. Credit: BYD

[# Understand China EV’s Market

Real-time notifications when critical EV data is released

All important data in one place

2,000,000+ data points

Become a member](https://db.carnewschina.com/get-full-access)

[BYD Seal 08](https://carnewschina.com/topics/byd-seal-08/) electric flagship sedans secured substantial market volume following the official [commercial model rollout](https://carnewschina.com/2026/07/02/byd-seal-08-flagship-sedan-launch-china/) on July 2, 2026. The 5.15-meter luxury passenger vehicle altered segment expectations by introducing advanced hardware architectures at a base entry price of 196,900 yuan (28,980 USD). Initial dealership tracking indicates immediate acceleration of the order.

The underlying vehicle architecture incorporates an 800-volt charging system and the specialised DiSus-A air suspension setup. Built on a 3,030 mm wheelbase, the sedan measures 5,150 mm long, 1,999 mm wide, and 1,505 mm tall. This technical configuration optimises chassis control metrics and power management efficiency during rapid charging cycles. Pure electric configurations account for over 65% of all selections, compared with hybrid alternatives.

### Order intake metrics

Channel intelligence compiled by Xueqiu [BYD](https://carnewschina.com/topics/byd/) researcher [Andy Ding](https://xueqiu.com/u/9080548226) indicates that cumulative locked orders reached approximately 65,000 units in just over 30 hours post-launch. This total includes 40,000 blind bookings registered since June 12, as well as new orders exceeding 25,000 units following the price disclosure. These tracking metrics carry an estimated 10% to 15% margin of error.

Customer choice focuses on the pure-electric all-wheel-drive flagship configuration, delivering a 905 km CLTC range. This high-performance layout utilises dual motors to output a combined 510 kW (694 hp) to achieve a 3.3-second sprint. High-spec variations featuring this integrated system experience the longest delivery wait times due to constraints on specialised components.

Plug-in hybrid configurations represent the remaining segment of the initial order intake. These variants deliver a 400 km pure electric capability alongside a combined 1,660 km operational range ceiling. This dual powertrain strategy splits consumer options between pure electric and hybrid architectures across the retail network.

### Battery supply boundaries

Manufacturing acceleration for the [second-generation Blade battery](https://carnewschina.com/2026/06/30/byd-blade-battery-production-surge-happy-problem/) cell lines marks the start of an active production expansion phase to meet incoming demand. Assembly facility capacity is hovering at 8,000 vehicles per month, creating a temporary backlog against incoming order volume. Mainstream dealership delivery queues currently span 2 to 3 months for popular trims.

The manufacturer implemented double-shift production schedules to increase factory output and ease delivery pressure. This operational directive aims to accelerate line throughput to balance incoming vehicle bookings. Scaling these specialised battery production assets remains the primary focus during the initial vehicle rollout period.

### Lineup sales context

[China EV DataTracker data](https://data.carnewschina.com/sales/brand/byd) from May 2026 establishes the baseline volume performance across the corporate lineup prior to this flagship launch. The Sealion 06 led retail deliveries at 18,856 units, while the Yuan UP secured 17,043 units. Concurrently, the Song Pro DM-i registered 15,497 sales during the month.

The Qin Plus EV and Dolphin models contributed 12,971 and 12,819 units to the monthly brand total.
